and explained it It's interesting that I see this again and again and again That when I actually go out and meet the people who are using the straight line system They seem to almost all of them make this like the same major mistake in terms of how they Spread the sale out on the line in terms of you know Whether it's you know trying to stretch things out into too many calls like a three-quals It should be a two-quall or a one-quall that should be a two-quall or better still a one-quall It's better off as a hybrid one-quall, which I'm gonna go through today along with some other stuff in terms of structure but I think there's a lack of of deeper understanding here of One of the fundamental principles that Guides the entire straight line that really led to its creation and that is the path of least resistance In other words everything that I do When I sell Every word out of my mouth every every move I make every strategic Loop I do a painos little nips and tucks Well, it's always part of what I consider at least in my own mind at the moment the path of least resistance I try to make it easiest for myself to close the sale and there are certain very obvious things That you should be doing that yet and I explain them yet when I go out into the field and I speak to the people who use the straight line a lot of them Honestly was most of them 90% aren't doing it and then when I show them They're like, oh my god, I can't believe you yet So obvious like you're right and then as soon as they implement that their sales literally soar to a whole new level So it's not like it's not this is a small distinction that you know this is something that when you get this right It has a dramatic impact on your closing rate So I want to dig into it much more deeply today and and cover And let me draw out the straight line for you open your clothes, right and Remember right the perfect sale Is represented by the straight line, right? That's everything you say everything you do Every word that comes out of your mouth Every document you hand someone Every website you direct them to me That's it your closing through a webinar where you have your customer sitting in front of computer sharing your screen with them You do that because it's designed to help you close the sale We don't do things for the sake of doing them with the straight line, right? There's no frills and no extra trimmings. We do everything for a purposeful Outcome here and that outcome is is to get your prospect to say yes Now depending on what type of call you're on that yes could see could simply be mean setting up An appointment with you for the next call So there's a close on every single call and when I say call That call could be in person or over the phone and In person it could be you going out into the field and knocking on doors are going to preset appointments or frankly It could be you sitting inside a retail type of business where people are coming into your store whether it's from ads or Incoming from a call call whatever they come to you and you then take you through presentation in that case from the moment They step in your door every bit of your presentation Every bit of the actually the room is designed and lay out is really structured as such to create this this path of lease Resistance for you to close as many sales as possible and I've been through this with the you know for one great examples Is when them right vacation metaphor a bunch of years might be a really in-depth About three months long training with them where I really help the redeveloped a device a hole into our sales And from who's great to spot what they just want to take it to the next level right and in that case what's happening? It's not that you just can ask questions in the right order And the right tonality from big picture to small not a basic and basic that's not just that It's so many things beside that that allow you basically to Essentially control the flow of the entire encounter from start to finish every last bit of it So let me get specific now. That's what the straight line is and this model represents the person who plays along with you essentially Without any any any like, you know, you know push back at all They allow you to use your pre-planned structure from start to finish And they just say yes the whole way and give you the their money or credit card them, right? That's a perfect straight line sale. Everyone get that. That's a deep explanation and the reason that's important Is because like everything else in life. We always have you always have this perfection At the center of what we do we don't we strive for perfection in everything we do in life When we know we're gonna drive a car we drive it perfectly We attempt to drive it perfectly, right? We're in a relationship with another human being we try to be a perfect partner Right when we raise our children want to raise our children perfectly we play a sport We try to be perfect in what we do, but that's not the way it really plans out in life, right? Doesn't work that way But how long you try there are no perfect relations perfect perfect parents perfect sports figures even Roger Federer will lose in the fucking finals and Tiger Woods can go up the deck in the real world Not how hard you try no matter how many times you play that vision out Let's remember it you want to visualize yourself succeeding before you ever do you visualize them already close the sale, right? but in a you have this perfect strategy that you've spent a long time as Strategic preparation creating that perfect line the shortest distance between two points of strength to take you from where you are to where you want to be That's what your planning is all about It's in the hopes that you'll have this perfect client who will be a yes machine the whole way and then because everything in life is like sort of like No matter how hard you try to duplicate it right you know if you have on a picture is a great example This if you have a perfect picture Right, and then you download it and then you download it again You get what ends up having over time the quality slowly degrades the image gets a little bit less clear Right because in just like as things keep going or repeating they start to sort of devolve a bit from perfection The copy is never quite as good as the original so on some other ones Why I'm just trying to choose different metaphors so I can hit everybody here because you gotta get this point All right, this is the essence of what you really do is a sales person So what's happening is the reason this initial sale must be so perfectly planned out Is that because what's gonna happen is it's not gonna end up working out that way in the real world when you go after and try Deploy the straight line. Do you use a two call system or three now? It's just you're thinking correctly there Especially if you have a three-call system and you're thinking about collapsing it to a two-call I would try everything you could to do that Remember systems that are over two calls are Typically because there are multiple decision makers involved and you have to crowd a bunch of different yeses from the number people That's the real reason why they're a longer sales cycles or this specific to the product that is complex and never never requires the Integrating into other parts of the company like I just signed up with a really great company to help me do some Customer service stuff right advanced targeted customer service. It took four or five calls For them to close the deal with us But that's because if I say yes, it's no simple affair It's gonna touch every part of my business so I have to go through layers of people checking cross-checking That's what longer sales cycles of four If you're a simple business we have provider service it boom it stands alone It brings in leaves or does this you fucking should do it in two calls no more The third calls for the ones that you couldn't get on the second try, but you'll want to be asking for that order The question is though is how do you balance out that? That contradiction is a contradiction here right what is it well very simply? you Want to try to avoid the problem of getting in touch with the same person twice But two call systems are more effective because you've been able to get into rapport They've seen state your real good sense of information The second call is just a warmer call because once someone's spoken to you once that represents You know that's a notch okay, you're real because now you're back again So there's more trust develop right there's reasons why two calls work better There's sort of chance to become more certain About both your product if you send them out the correct information it really looks makes your product great You also makes you look good in your company You're starting to move up the certainty scale for the three tens before the second call even begins They entered the second call at a higher level of certainty Cold call if you do a cold call and trying to close in that cold call Let's go back to what certainty scale now watch What happens one ten on a cold cold cold? It's because this is what everyone seems to miss is that there's a psychological tug-of-war going on in those first 15 to 20 seconds and When you it's like you it's like Julia or what's that stuff? It's Steven's a hot title, right? Where it's like you use someone else's power against themselves, right to win the war or the battle so to speak So what you're doing is is like you're pushing Pushing the guy push it with the best this the best that the best that will work We help people just like you push push push and the guys like all mine. Let me say this All things being equal If you had an unlimited amount of names to call And you were sitting behind a desk phone or on a computer phone, right? Headset on you can make 300 calls a day I fucking promise you and I'll I I bet you a million bucks right now at least 300 calls a day at least every fucking day If you are cold cold you have an unlimited number of people And you're using a two-call system and not getting caught up on 30 minute closing calls Now if you're hybridting you might maybe make 200 calls a day depending on the numbers, but it's in the hundreds. It's not 50 It's not 75. It's not 100. It's in the hundreds to fact and The one sure fire away to close more sales is to make more calls, buddy. That's just the way it is Nothing increases productivity like hard work Everything else is required too, but that's the bottom line.
